events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist Brian May was born on this day in 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the iconic Queen guitarist, Brian May! What's your favourite riff or solo from the man himself?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones frontman Mick Jagger was born on this day in 1943.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Royalty
angles.copy: Still strutting his stuff at 83! Wishing a very happy birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger. What's your top Stones track?

events.title: 45 years since MTV launched
events.blurb: Music Television, MTV, launched on this day in 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the first music video ever aired.

angles.name: Changing the game
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever! Remember the first video they played? 'Video Killed The Radio Star' by The Buggles. Did it really kill the radio star? We think not!

events.title: 32 years of Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe'
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on this day in 1994, featuring hits like 'Supersonic' and 'Live Forever'.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Thirty-two years ago, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and Britpop was never the same. What's your favourite track from this legendary debut?

events.title: 57 years since Woodstock
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, began on this day in 1969 in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: 57 years since the mud and music of Woodstock! Three days that defined a generation. What artists from that era still resonate with you today?

events.title: Remembering Elvis Presley
events.blurb: On this day in 1977, the world lost the King of Rock 'n' Roll, Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock 'n' Roll, Elvis Presley, who left us on this day in 1977. His music and legacy continue to inspire.

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: On this day in 1996, influential rapper Tupac Shakur tragically passed away, he would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Tupac's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering the profound impact of Tupac Shakur, who tragically left us on this day in 1996. His words and music live on.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: The legendary guitarist Jimi Hendrix passed away on this day in 1970, he would have been 83 this year.

angles.name: Guitar Genius
angles.copy: On this day in 1970, we lost a true guitar visionary, Jimi Hendrix. His groundbreaking sound continues to inspire generations.

events.title: 57 years of The Beatles' 'Abbey Road'
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic album 'Abbey Road' on this day in the UK in 1969, featuring tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Here Comes the Sun'.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: That zebra crossing! 57 years ago today, The Beatles released 'Abbey Road'. What's your favourite track from this masterpiece?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: The Pretenders' frontwoman Chrissie Hynde was born on this day in 1951.

angles.name: Pretenders Power
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon and the powerhouse voice of The Pretenders.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher was born on this day in 1972.

angles.name: Mad for it!
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the legendary Liam Gallagher! What's your favourite Oasis anthem delivered by his unmistakable vocals?

events.title: 55 years of John Lennon's 'Imagine' album
events.blurb: John Lennon released his iconic album 'Imagine' on this day in the US in 1971, featuring the timeless title track.

angles.name: A Song for Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-five years ago, John Lennon gave us the 'Imagine' album. Its message of peace and unity is as powerful today as it was in 1971.

events.title: 25 years since the first iPod was announced
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the original iPod on this day in 2001, revolutionising how we listen to music. It was released to the public on November 10, 2001.

angles.name: Music in your pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? 25 years ago today, Apple announced the device that put 1,000 songs in your pocket and changed music consumption forever.

events.title: Global James Bond Day
events.blurb: Global James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the 1962 premiere of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No'.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy Global James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to 007 and his incredible 60+ year cinematic legacy. What's your favourite Bond film or theme tune?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles co-founder John Lennon was born on this day in 1940.

angles.name: A Visionary Remembered
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have turned 86 today. His music and message of peace continue to resonate globally.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The iconic British comedian, actor, and writer John Cleese was born on this day in 1939.

angles.name: Comedy Legend
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 87th birthday to the brilliant John Cleese! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, a true master of British comedy.

events.title: 58 years of The Beatles' 'White Album'
events.blurb: The Beatles released their self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', on this day in 1968.

angles.name: Experimental Masterpiece
angles.copy: The Beatles' 'White Album' turns 58 today! A sprawling, experimental masterpiece that showed every facet of the Fab Four. What's your favourite track?
